artifact.governing_rule: Every named candidate receives a deterministic structural disposition. Missing effect evidence remains unscored, never zero.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat supported depression or anxiety.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Condition and modality require separate records.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Reduce, replace, retime, or discontinue an offending medication under the appropriate prescriber.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Drug, indication, change type, taper, comparator, and harms are medication-specific.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat unhealthy alcohol or substance use.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Alcohol and each substance class require separate withdrawal gates and treatment records.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat supported anemia or iron deficiency and evaluate its cause.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Treatment and cause evaluation are different routes.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat overt thyroid disease.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and thyroiditis cannot share one treatment record.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Replete confirmed vitamin B12 deficiency.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Route, etiology, neurologic involvement, comparator, and source effect are unresolved.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat a supported glucose, renal, hepatic, or electrolyte cause.
candidate_reviews.rationale: This is a diagnostic routing category across unrelated disease families.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat confirmed obstructive sleep apnea.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Positive airway pressure is bounded enough to retain.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat supported insomnia or another confirmed sleep disorder.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Retain CBT-I for insomnia; other disorders require separate records.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat pain and restore function.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Retain one current pain-condition and intervention exemplar.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat clinically significant menopause-related symptoms.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Each symptom cluster and treatment requires its own benefit and harm record.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Treat confirmed symptomatic hypogonadism.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Specialist-guided care does not name an executable treatment.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Increase sleep opportunity.
candidate_reviews.rationale: A bounded sleep-extension trial is executable.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Stabilize sleep and wake timing.
candidate_reviews.rationale: A regularity intervention is executable when adequate opportunity is preserved.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Correct morning and evening light timing.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Morning and evening light are different exposures.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Move caffeine earlier or reduce it.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Retain timing; generic reduction lacks a baseline-relative taper.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Reduce nonclinical alcohol use.
candidate_reviews.rationale: A four-week alcohol-free trial is reproducible; generic reduction is not.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Add protected recovery from work, caregiving, or training.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Work, caregiving, and obligation removal require separate exposure definitions; training recovery routes through candidate 23.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Increase daily walking or routine movement.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Retain easy walking; routine movement is not a reproducible dose.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Begin progressive aerobic exercise.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Executable and the highest-priority future mapping candidate.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Begin progressive resistance training.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Executable with a bounded progression.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Interrupt prolonged sedentary time.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Clear acute intervention and comparator.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Reduce excessive training load.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Ordinary deload and persistent deterioration require elective and diagnostic routes.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Schedule recurring meaningful one-to-one contact.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Retain with one selected person, cadence, duration, and safety constraints.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Join a recurring group or community activity.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Require active participation in one recurring group.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Protect time with a close relationship.
candidate_reviews.rationale: A close relationship is a subtype of meaningful one-to-one contact.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Resume a valued or enjoyable activity.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Enjoyment and value expression are different constructs.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Start a bounded mastery or learning project.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Retain with a completion criterion and effort ceiling.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Add recurring contribution, mentoring, or service activity.
candidate_reviews.rationale: Instantiate one specific role per patient.

candidate_reviews.original_label: Remove or delegate one recurring low-value obligation.
candidate_reviews.rationale: This is one implementation of protected recovery, not an independent effect family.

actions.title: Behavioral activation for supported depression
actions.atomic_definition: Individual behavioral activation delivered as treatment for supported depression.
actions.dose: Diagnosis-specific treatment plan; source-bound session dose pending.
actions.comparator: Waitlist, usual care, or named control.
actions.source_population: Adults with supported depression.
actions.follow_up: Reassess at the response-period boundary or sooner for intolerance, harm, or clinical deterioration.

effect.blocker: Depressive symptom severity is not Aleron Mood in points of 10.

confidence.rationale: No calibrated confidence rubric or qualified evidence review is attached.

burden.rationale: Time, cost, complexity, and opportunity cost have not been characterized.

urgency.rationale: Route is known, but urgency has not been clinically characterized.

evidence_trace.limitations: Not a governed evidence atom or qualified clinical review.

actions.title: Oral iron for nonanemic iron deficiency with fatigue
actions.atomic_definition: Oral ferrous sulfate providing 80 mg elemental iron daily for 12 weeks, with laboratory follow-up.
actions.dose: 80 mg elemental iron daily for 12 weeks.
actions.comparator: Placebo.
actions.source_population: Menstruating women aged 18 to 53 years with fatigue, ferritin below 50 µg/L, and hemoglobin above 12.0 g/dL.
actions.follow_up: Reassess at the response-period boundary or sooner for intolerance, harm, or clinical deterioration.

effect.blocker: Percentage fatigue reduction cannot be converted without source score distribution and an approved crosswalk.

actions.source_population: Patients with confirmed iron deficiency requiring etiologic evaluation.
actions.follow_up: Reassess at the response-period boundary or sooner for intolerance, harm, or clinical deterioration.

actions.source_population: Adults aged 35 to 64 years with documented habitual short sleep and no unresolved sleep disorder.
actions.follow_up: Reassess at the response-period boundary or sooner for intolerance, harm, or clinical deterioration.

actions.atomic_definition: Take one 5 to 10 minute break after each 90 minutes of sustained work on at least 5 days weekly.

actions.title: Complete one values-aligned activity
actions.atomic_definition: Complete one patient-selected activity that expresses a named personal value.
actions.dose: One predefined activity weekly for 6 weeks.
actions.comparator: Existing routine without the scheduled activity.
actions.source_population: Adults able to name one value and feasible action with a realistic effort ceiling.
actions.follow_up: Reassess at the response-period boundary or sooner for intolerance, harm, or clinical deterioration.

effect.blocker: Psychological wellbeing and valued living are adjacent to, not identical with, Meaning.

confidence.rationale: No calibrated confidence rubric or qualified evidence review is attached.

burden.rationale: Time, cost, complexity, and opportunity cost have not been characterized.

harm.rationale: Clinical harms, contraindications, and monitoring burden have not been reviewed for promotion.

feasibility.rationale: Access, capacity, schedule, and support are patient-specific and unresolved.

urgency.rationale: Route is known, but urgency has not been clinically characterized.

evidence_trace.limitations: Not a governed evidence atom or qualified clinical review.

actions.title: Bounded mastery project
actions.atomic_definition: Start one patient-selected learning or skill project with a concrete deliverable and fixed scope.
actions.dose: Two sessions weekly of 30 to 60 minutes for 8 weeks, capped at 2 hours weekly.
actions.comparator: Existing routine without the project.
actions.source_population: Adults with accessible resources, realistic prerequisites, protected time, and a stop rule.
actions.follow_up: Reassess at the response-period boundary or sooner for intolerance, harm, or clinical deterioration.

effect.blocker: No general-adult intervention evidence or crosswalk supports Meaning or Mind.

confidence.rationale: No calibrated confidence rubric or qualified evidence review is attached.

burden.rationale: Time, cost, complexity, and opportunity cost have not been characterized.

harm.rationale: Clinical harms, contraindications, and monitoring burden have not been reviewed for promotion.

feasibility.rationale: Access, capacity, schedule, and support are patient-specific and unresolved.

urgency.rationale: Route is known, but urgency has not been clinically characterized.

evidence_trace.limitations: Not a governed evidence atom or qualified clinical review.

actions.title: Recurring contribution or service
actions.atomic_definition: Perform one defined contribution, mentoring, or service role for a named recipient or organization.
actions.dose: One session weekly of 60 to 120 minutes for 8 weeks.
actions.comparator: Existing routine without the service role.
actions.source_population: Adults with a safe role, clear boundaries, schedule control, and an exit option.
actions.follow_up: Reassess at the response-period boundary or sooner for intolerance, harm, or clinical deterioration.

effect.blocker: Volunteering evidence is heterogeneous and causally weak, with no Meaning crosswalk.

confidence.rationale: No calibrated confidence rubric or qualified evidence review is attached.

burden.rationale: Time, cost, complexity, and opportunity cost have not been characterized.

feasibility.rationale: Access, capacity, schedule, and support are patient-specific and unresolved.

urgency.rationale: Route is known, but urgency has not been clinically characterized.

actions.title: Protected recovery by obligation removal
actions.atomic_definition: Remove, delegate, or reduce one optional recurring obligation and reserve the released time for recovery.
actions.dose: Change one obligation within 2 weeks and release at least 30 minutes weekly for 4 weeks.
actions.comparator: Continue the obligation unchanged.
actions.source_population: Adults with authority to change a genuinely optional or safely delegable obligation.
actions.follow_up: Reassess at the response-period boundary or sooner for intolerance, harm, or clinical deterioration.

effect.blocker: Job demands, burnout, engagement, and recovered time are not Aleron Energy or Meaning.

confidence.rationale: No calibrated confidence rubric or qualified evidence review is attached.
